content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Patrick Lippert 
Hayden Campaign committee 
1337 Santa Monica Mall, Ste. 311 
Santa Monica, CA 
90401 
Dear Mr. Lippert: 
July 23, 1986 
Re: FPPC No. A-86-236 
This is to confirm the advice I provided to you by telephone 
concerning the campaign disclosure obligations of a committee 
primarily formed to qualify a measure for the statewide ballot. 
Please forgive my delay in providing you with this written 
confirmation. 
QUESTIONS 
We discussed the following questions: 
(1) 
When must the qualification committee's first campaign 
disclosure statement be filed? 
(2) 
What are the requirements with regard to identifying 
the controlling candidates and proponents on the Statement of 
Organization and for verification of committee campaign 
disclosure statements for a committee which is controlled by 
more than one candidate or state measure proponent. 
ANSWERS 
(1) 
Gov. Code section 84200.5(f) provides that committees 
primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of a 
ballot measure shall file a statement no later than 35 days 
after the deadline for filing petitions, OR 35 days after the 
date of notification that the measure has qualified or failed to 
qualify for the ballot, whichever is earlier. 
The deadline for filing petitions in connection with the 
qualification effort is July 26, 1986. 
However, the committee 
completed the circulation process and submitted the petitions on 
an earlier date in June in order to meet the deadline for 
placement on the November ballot. 
The submission of petitions 
early does not change the actual "deadline for filing 
petitions," which, in this case is July 26, 1986. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 .. Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Mr. Lippert 
July 23, 1986 
Page Two 
Notification that the measure had qualified was issued by 
the Secretary of State on June 26, 1986. 
Therefore, since the 
date of notification is earlier than the deadline for filing 
petitions, the statement required by Gov. Code section 
84200.5(f) must be filed no later than 35 days after the date of 
notification. 
(Thirty-five days after the date of notification 
is July 31, 1986.) 
(2) 
The qualification committee about which you are 
inquiring is controlled by 20 ballot measure proponents, some of 
whom are candidates. 
FPPC Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18430 sets out 
requirements for the information which must be provided on the 
Statement of Organization of a committee which is controlled by 
more than one candidate, and also provides a procedure for 
verification of campaign statements of a committee controlled by 
more than one candidate. 
with regard to identification of the proponents on the 
committee's statement of Organization and campaign disclosure 
statements, I advised you that: 
(1) 
The name of each controlling candidate and 
proponent must be listed on the Statement of Organization 
(Form 410). 
(You indicated that the committee's statement 
of Organization would be amended to comply with this 
requirement.) ; 
(2) 
The name of each controlling candidate and 
proponent must be listed on the cover sheet of every 
campaign statement filed by the committee; 
(3) 
Each controlling candidate must list the name of 
the committee, indicating that it is a controlled committee 
of that candidate, on the cover sheet of every campaign 
statement filed by the candidate; 
(4) 
The campaign statements of the controlled 
committee cannot be combined or consolidated with the 
campaign statements of any controlling candidate. 
with regard to verification of the committee's campaign 
disclosure statements, one of the controlling candidates or 
proponents may sign and verify the statements on behalf of all 


Mr. Lippert 
July 23, 1986 
Page Three 
the controlling candidates and proponents. 
I suggested to you, 
and you agreed, that the campaign statements should be signed 
and verified by the candidate who is most involved in the 
committee's activities; that is, the candidate who has the most 
significant influence on the actions or decisions of the 
committee. 
Enclosed for your information is a copy of FPPC Regulation 2 
Cal. Adm. Code section 18430. 
I hope this adequately responds to your questions. 
Please 
call me if you have any further questions. 
JP:ch 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
/"1 
f; 
. 
1/,.:-( 7;-; ,'/! / ./C/ 
?<!/7~7.~ ,/,-< .. A: .. t:.. 
~c.C L Li. 
v'Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief, Technical 
Assistance & Analysis Division
